opening_angle: Connect Podium's SOC engineering needs to Conner’s strength in building deployable detection systems and automation that reduce manual toil — emphasize operational reliability over theoretical security
paragraph_focus: Direct alignment with detection engineering and SOC escalation needs using concrete examples (endpoint remediation, SIEM tuning, incident playbooks)
Highlight multi-source data integration and correlation work as foundation for alert fidelity improvements
Showcase API-driven tooling and runbook automation that directly address Podium’s integration and containment requirements
Reinforce IC-focused contribution: shipping, documenting, building guardrails — no leadership fluff
voice_controls: Use 'I built' instead of 'I helped' to own deliverables
Replace vague claims like 'high-impact security outcomes' with specific actions: 'reduced MTTR', 'standardized remediation workflows'
Avoid 'passionate about security' — use 'I ship reliable systems that hold up in production'
Use Podium’s language: 'deployable', 'operational rigor', 'false positive reduction'
claims_to_avoid: Mentioning people management or formal leadership
Generic AI hype — only reference AI where evidence shows auditability and safe deployment
Inventing metrics not in source material (e.g., 'cut MTTR by 40%')
Abstract mission statements without technical grounding
